topic: Case study: transformation effects on k-NN, SVM, tree-based
key_concepts: Showing the empirical impact of applying scaling vs leaving data raw across three different model families.
For k-NN and SVM: Accuracy improves dramatically after standardization because large-scale features no longer dominate the Euclidean space.
For tree-based: Accuracy remains identical pre- and post-scaling, proving their immunity.
Takeaway decision: Always scale before distance/gradient models, but skip the computational overhead for tree-based architectures.
examples: A dataset with 'Salary' (large values) and 'Years Experience' (small values).
exclusions: Do not show block code. Keep it descriptive.

figures.caption: Dampak skala fitur terhadap akurasi berbagai keluarga model algoritma
figures.description: A bar chart comparing model accuracy (k-NN, SVM, Random Forest) on raw versus standard-scaled data.
